title: Mastering SMILE: Precision Parameters in Refractive Surgery
summary: Small Incision Lenticule Extraction (SMILE) represents the pinnacle of minimally invasive refractive surgery. This guide details the essential parameters, nomograms, and clinical pearls for optimizing outcomes in myopia and astigmatism correction.

data.title: Mastering SMILE: Precision Parameters in Refractive Surgery
data.summary: Small Incision Lenticule Extraction (SMILE) represents the pinnacle of minimally invasive refractive surgery. This guide details the essential parameters, nomograms, and clinical pearls for optimizing outcomes in myopia and astigmatism correction.

sections.title: Definition & Indications
sections.content: SMILE is a femtosecond laser-assisted refractive procedure where a refractive lenticule is created within the corneal stroma and extracted through a small peripheral incision (typically 2-4mm). Indicated for myopia up to -10.00 D and myopic astigmatism up to -5.00 D (cyl) where corneal thickness is sufficient.

sections.title: Surgical Extraction Protocol
content: Step 2: Femtosecond laser generation of posterior then anterior lenticule surfaces
Step 3: Incision creation at 12 o'clock position
Step 4: Dissection of anterior and posterior planes using a micro-spatula
Step 5: Lenticule extraction using micro-forceps (e.g., Akahoshi or similar)

sections.title: Key Clinical Pearls
content: Ensure patient fixation is stable throughout the laser sequence to avoid induced astigmatism.
Residual stromal thickness (RST) must be calculated post-extraction; aim for > 250 μm.
SMILE is less sensitive to dry eye compared to LASIK due to lack of a long circumferential flap hinge.
Centration is based on the pupillary center, not the corneal vertex.

content.explanation: S: Suction stable; M: Micro-spatula care; I: Incision precision; L: Lenticule integrity; E: Extraction technique.
